macro_segments.title: The Minimum Ketubah Amount and the Mishnah's Attribution to Rabbi Meir

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah on the 100 and 200 Zuz Standard Amounts
Rabbi Meir's View: Amounts Less Than Standard Constitute Be'ilat Zenut (Fornication)
Distinguishing Censure from Voiding: Does Writing Less Than Standard Void the Ketubah?
Rabbi Yehuda's Alternative: Writing Standard Amount with Receipt to Reduce Effectively
Gemara's Attribution: Mishnah's First Section is Rabbi Meir

macro_segments.title: Liens on Property and the Mishnah's Second Section's Attribution to Rabbi Yehuda

micro_segments.title: Mishnah's Seifa: Liens Cannot Be Limited to Specific Fields Despite Husband's Writing
Rabbi Yehuda's Principle: Achrayut (Liens) Assumed in Any Written Loan Document
Baraita on Lost Loan Documents: Rabbi Meir vs. Sages on Liens Requirement
The Five Categories Collected Only from Unencumbered Property
Reconciling the Contradiction: Mishnah's Seifa Follows Rabbi Yehuda

macro_segments.title: Rape and Captivity: Consequences for Wife of Yisrael vs. Wife of Kohen

micro_segments.title: Father of Shmuel's Position: Even Captive Wife of Yisrael Becomes Forbidden Due to Possible Willingness
Rav's Challenge: The Ketubah Language States Redemption and Return
Distinction Between Captivity and Rape: Captivity Permits Return to Husband
Rava's Principle: Coercion at Onset Vitiates Later Willingness
Baraita on Captive Women: Those Taken by Bandits Can Return; Fear-Based Actions Don't Constitute Willingness
King vs. Bandit: Achashverosh Scenario Differs from Ben Netzer (Bandit-King)

macro_segments.title: The Ketubah Obligation to Redeem Across Different Marriage Categories

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Case: Widow or Divorced Woman Married to Kohen Gadol—Redemption Still Applies
Distinction: Ongoing Marital Obligations Don't Apply to Illicit Marriages; Redemption Differs
Rava's Refinement: Redemption Obligation Only When Captivity Prevents Marriage, Not Pre-Existing Impediments
Baraita on Vow Creating Obligation to Divorce: Redemption Debate Between Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Yehoshua
Rabbi Nassan's Query: Does Timing of Vow Relative to Captivity Matter for Redemption Obligation?
Gemara's Proposal: Debate Parallels Wife of Kohen Case—Whether Obligation Persists When Divorce Required
